title: शुक्र स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री शुक्र स्तोत्रम् ॥

हिमकुन्दमृणालाभं दैत्यानां परमं गुरुम्।
सर्वशास्त्रप्रवक्तारं भार्गवं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ १॥

श्वेतवर्णं महाबुद्धिं श्वेतवस्त्रविभूषितम्।
दण्डचर्मधरं शान्तं प्रणमामि शुक्रं गुरुम्॥ २॥

वृषभतुलाराशीशं शुक्रवारप्रियं सदा।
काव्यविद्याप्रदातारं प्रणमामि भृगुसुतम्॥ ३॥

मृतसञ्जीवनीविद्याधरं दैत्यगुरुं प्रभुम्।
सर्वसुखप्रदातारं प्रणमामि ग्रहेश्वरम्॥ ४॥

विवाहसौभाग्यकरं कामदेवसमप्रभम्।
सर्वकलाप्रियं देवं प्रणमामि शुक्राचार्यम्॥ ५॥

शुक्रस्तोत्रमिदं पुण्यं यः पठेच्छुक्रवासरे।
शुक्रपीडा न बाधेत सर्वसौभाग्यमाप्नुयात्॥

॥ इति श्री शुक्र स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
benefits: Sacred Shukra Stotram for devotion and spiritual growth
Invokes blessings of Shukra in all forms
Invokes divine protection and planetary peace
Traditionally recited for removal of afflictions and bestows auspiciousness
Ideal for Shukra graha shanti and worship
description: Shukra Stotram (शुक्र स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise dedicated to the Nine Planets (Navagraha). Rooted in Purana Tradition, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Devotees recite this prayer to invoke blessings of Shukra in all forms and grant divine protection and planetary peace. It is also said to remove afflictions and bestow auspiciousness. This prayer is traditionally recited on Friday (Shukravar) and during Diwali to invoke divine grace. Comprising 5 verses, it can be completed in approximately 3 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Shukra Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Shukra Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Sacred hymn for Shukra graha shanti.

seoFaqs.question: How is this Shukra Stotram different from the longer Shukra Stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: This shorter variant of the Shukra Stotram is a compact recitation that shares the opening verse Hima-kunda-mrinala-abham daityanam paramam gurum with the longer eight-verse Skanda Purana Shukra Stotra but condenses the descriptive verses into a five to six shloka format. It adds explicit references to Shukra as shvetavarnam mahabuddhim (white-complexioned and great in intellect), shvetavastra-vibhushitam (ornamented in white garments), danda-charma-dharam shantam (holding staff and shield, peaceful), and as the lord of Vrishabha and Tula rashis. It is preferred for daily three-minute Friday recitation while the longer Skanda Purana version is reserved for elaborate navagraha homas. (Sources: Stotra Samhita; Vedadhara Shukra texts.)

seoFaqs.question: Why does Shukra rule Vrishabha (Taurus) and Tula (Libra)?
seoFaqs.answer: In Vedic astrology each planet rules one or two of the twelve rashis based on its essential nature, and Shukra (Venus) is assigned the second sign Vrishabha (Taurus) and the seventh sign Tula (Libra). Vrishabha is the natural second house of accumulated wealth, food, family and possessions — the resource-base of life, governed by Shukra's karakatva of bhoga (enjoyment) and Lakshmi-vaibhava. Tula is the natural seventh house of marriage, partnerships, business agreements and balance — governed by Shukra's karakatva of relationships, kala (the arts) and beauty. Shukra is exalted in Meena (Pisces) and debilitated in Kanya (Virgo). The Shukra Stotram is recited especially when Shukra in these rashis is afflicted. (Sources: BV Raman Vedic Astrology; AstroSage Venus.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Mrita-sanjivani vidya associated with Shukracharya?
seoFaqs.answer: The Mrita-sanjivani vidya is the secret science of reviving the dead, obtained by Shukracharya through severe tapas to Lord Shiva who is pleased with him alone among the gurus. With this vidya Shukracharya was able to repeatedly resurrect asuras slain by the devas in the deva-asura wars, prolonging the cosmic conflict and forcing Brihaspati to send his son Kacha as a disciple to learn the vidya from Shukra (the famous Kacha-Devayani story of the Mahabharata Adi Parva). The Sanjeevani Mantra is preserved in tantric tradition and is associated with Shukra's astrological role as the karaka of revival, regeneration and rejuvenation. (Sources: Mahabharata Adi Parva 71-76; Devi Bhagavata Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Shukra the guru of the asuras and Brihaspati the guru of the devas?
seoFaqs.answer: Hindu cosmology divides the celestial intelligence between two great preceptors: Brihaspati (Jupiter), son of Maharishi Angiras, became the guru of the devas; and Shukra (Venus), son of Maharishi Bhrigu, became the guru of the asuras. Their rivalry shapes much of puranic history. The deeper allegorical meaning is that Brihaspati represents dharma-shastra and the path of sattva-guna while Shukra represents the kavya-shastra (the art of statecraft and worldly enjoyment) and the path of rajas-tamas mixed with deep devotion. In jyotisha, this is reflected in Brihaspati being the karaka of dharma, jnana and moksha, and Shukra being the karaka of bhoga, kala and kama. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 8; Wikipedia Shukra; AstroSage Venus karakatva.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the connection between Shukra Stotram and Lakshmi worship?
seoFaqs.answer: Although Shukra and Lakshmi are technically distinct deities, jyotisha and bhakti tradition link them closely because Shukra is the karaka of all that Lakshmi represents — wealth, beauty, vehicles, jewellery, marital harmony, comforts and aishvarya. Many devotees therefore recite the Shukra Stotram before commencing the Vaibhava Lakshmi Vrat (the eleven-week Friday Lakshmi vow), the Vara Mahalakshmi Vrat (the Friday before Shravana Purnima), and the daily Shukravar Lakshmi puja. Friday is sacred to both. The Shukra Stotram opens the planetary path while the Lakshmi Ashtakam, Sri Suktam or Lakshmi Sahasranama follows as the deity-puja proper. (Sources: Drikpanchang Friday Lakshmi vrat; AstroSage Friday remedies.)

seoFaqs.question: What ailments and life problems are addressed by Shukra Stotram chanting?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional jyotisha and ayurveda prescribe Shukra Stotram for ailments arising from a weak or afflicted Venus: marital discord, delayed marriage, infidelity, divorce, infertility (especially in the female reproductive system since Shukra governs reproductive fluids and feminine vitality), kidney and urinary disorders, diabetes, skin and complexion issues, and venereal diseases. It is also chanted for relief from financial stagnation, loss of luxury and comforts, business failures in luxury goods, fashion, hospitality, music, dance and design industries, and for women seeking conception. Reciting it on Fridays after a bath in white-coloured items along with white sweets offering is the standard household practice. (Sources: AstroSage Venus karakatva; Drikpanchang Friday vrat.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Kanjanur Shukra Sthalam Navagraha temple?
seoFaqs.answer: Kanjanur, near Kumbakonam in Tamil Nadu, is one of the nine Navagraha temples (Navagraha Sthalam) of the Chola country and is the dedicated sthala of Shukra. The presiding deity is Agneeswarar (Shiva) but Shukra is enshrined within the moolasthana as the parivara devata, having merged with the lingam through penance. The famous Shukra abhishekam is performed during the Friday Shukra hora with nine types of grains, milk, and curd, while devotees chant the Shukra Stotram. Married couples desiring children, women seeking marital harmony, those facing Shukra-Shukra mahadasha-antardasha, and those with afflicted Shukra throng the temple, especially on the eight-yearly Shukra peyarchi (transit) days. (Sources: Kanjanur temple website; Wikipedia Navagraha Temples.)
